# Vendored third-party JavaScript

Tinted Studio is otherwise 100% first-party, no-build vanilla JS. The one
exception is the median-cut color quantizer used by the "extract scheme from
image" feature, vendored here rather than hand-ported so it tracks an actively
maintained upstream.

## `quantize.js`

- **What:** the MMCQ (modified median cut quantization) implementation that
  Lokesh Dhakar's [color-thief](https://github.com/lokesh/color-thief) uses.
  It is published as the standalone [`quantize`](https://github.com/olivierlesnicki/quantize)
  npm package. The CLI's `tinted-scheme-extractor` derives its palette from the
  Rust `color-thief` crate, itself a port of this same algorithm.
- **Source:** <https://github.com/olivierlesnicki/quantize> — `quantize.js`
- **Version:** 1.0.2
- **License:** MIT (see `LICENSE-quantize`). The file additionally bundles a
  small Protovis helper block (`pv.map`/`naturalOrder`/`sum`/`max`) that is
  BSD-licensed (Copyright 2010 Stanford Visualization Group) — its notice is
  preserved verbatim in the file header and reproduced in `LICENSE-quantize`.
  Both MIT and BSD are GPL-3.0-compatible and are on tinty's `about.toml`
  accepted list.

### Local modification

Exactly one change from upstream, at the end of the file: upstream ends with a
bare `module.exports = MMCQ.quantize`. Tinted Studio loads this as a plain
`<script>` (no bundler), where `module` is undefined and that line would throw,
so it is guarded and a `window.quantize` global is exposed. The algorithm and
both upstream license headers are untouched.

### Why this isn't in `LICENSES-THIRD-PARTY.md`

That file is generated by `cargo about` and only covers Rust/Cargo
dependencies. This vendored JS is invisible to `cargo about`/`cargo deny`, so
its attribution lives here (file header + `LICENSE-quantize` + this README) and
is shipped alongside the code in every `tinty studio` dump.

### Updating

Re-fetch `quantize.js` from the upstream tag, re-apply the single export patch
above, and re-run the studio's extraction parity check.
